question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?

fragility_points: One exact noon ET candle determines settlement.
A moderate overnight drawdown is sufficient to flip the result.
Residual source-surface mismatch risk remains until direct UI check at settlement.
key_assumptions: Binance UI settlement candle should align with Binance public BTCUSDT API/kline data.
No major overnight shock drives BTC down more than roughly 3.5% before the settlement minute.
Venue-specific operational anomalies do not distort the noon ET close.
main_logical_chain: Read contract rules to identify the governing venue, pair, field, threshold, and exact settlement time.
Check direct Binance BTCUSDT ticker and one-minute klines to measure current cushion over 72k and recent persistence above the threshold.
Apply an outside-view short-horizon crypto-volatility lens: Yes should be favored, but not treated as near-certain because a single adverse minute can settle No.
Conclude with a probability slightly below the 95.5% market price.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is comfortably above 72k on the governing Binance BTC/USDT venue, so Yes remains the base-rate favorite, but one-minute crypto settlement leaves real tail risk and argues for a probability slightly below the market.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality direct venue data and clear contract mechanics, but evidence independence is only medium because the key evidence is concentrated on the same governing venue.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still drop roughly 3.5% in under 24 hours, which is enough for No.
The market settles on one exact minute, making path fragility higher than the headline cushion suggests.
The contract text names the Binance UI chart surface, while verification used API data as proxy.
strongest_supports: Binance spot checked during the run was about 74.6k, around 2.6k above the threshold.
The most recent 1000 Binance one-minute closes sampled were all above 72k, with a minimum of 73,566.
The contract explicitly resolves from Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close data.
payload.timing_relevance: Research was performed on Apr 15 around 15:21-15:22 ET, while the governing candle is noon ET on Apr 16, so the market remained live with about 20.6 hours to settlement.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the Binance UI candle display and public API are perfectly surface-equivalent for settlement purposes.
Whether any late macro/crypto shock changes the downside distribution before noon ET.
payload.what_would_change_view: A Binance move toward 72k, evidence that the UI settlement candle differs from the API proxy, or a major overnight shock would reduce confidence materially.

fragility_points: A sharp macro or liquidation-driven selloff before settlement.
Binance-specific dislocation versus other BTC venues.
Compression of the current cushion to near zero before noon ET.

Binance price formation remains normal into the settlement minute.

main_logical_chain: Check the contract mechanics and confirm the governing source is Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at noon ET Apr 16.
Verify current Binance BTCUSDT price and recent 1-minute closes.
Measure the buffer above 72000 and compare it to plausible sub-24h downside volatility.
Conclude that Yes remains high probability but not certainty because one-minute exchange-specific crypto markets retain path risk.
payload.main_thesis: BTC/USDT on Binance is already meaningfully above 72000, so absent a sub-24h downside shock or venue-specific anomaly the noon ET Apr 16 candle is more likely than not to close above the strike.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary quality is good because the contract source and named underlying source were both checked directly; independence is only medium because these are complementary surfaces, not separate forecasts.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move more than 3-4% intraday, which is large enough to erase the current buffer.
Settlement depends on one exact minute and one exact exchange pair, creating venue-specific path risk.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules explicitly name Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute 12:00 PM ET candle close as the governing source.
Binance verification showed BTCUSDT around 74646.39, about 2646 points above the strike.

unresolved_ambiguities: No strong independent event calendar source was found to rule out all near-term macro shocks.
Exact frontend display/finality handling on Binance was not tested beyond API verification.
